enrichment.short_description: SuiteCRM is a free, open-source CRM for sales, marketing, and customer service, with optional paid hosting and support.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SuiteCRM: free open-source CRM with sales, marketing and support tools, plus optional managed hosting, support and SuiteASSURED plans.

features.title: Flexible data model
features.description: Build a tailored customer data repository that can be customized to fit specific business processes.

features.title: Reporting and dashboards
features.description: Get visibility into key sales, marketing and service metrics through built-in reporting tools.

features.title: Lead and opportunity management
features.description: Capture leads and track opportunities through the sales pipeline.

features.title: Marketing segmentation
features.description: Automate customer segmentation and run targeted marketing campaigns.

features.title: Customer service tools
features.description: Manage support cases in one place to reduce information silos across teams.

features.title: Open REST API
features.description: Connect SuiteCRM to third-party systems using an open API.

features.title: Third-party integrations
features.description: Plugin ecosystem covering Outlook, Xero, DocuSign, Mailchimp, QuickBooks, Zoom, WhatsApp and more.

pros: Core software is free and open source with no per-user licensing fees
Self-hosted deployment gives full ownership and customization freedom
Large plugin ecosystem for integrations (Outlook, Xero, DocuSign, Mailchimp, QuickBooks, Zoom)
SuiteCRM Ltd is ISO 9001 and ISO 27001 certified and a Crown Commercial Service supplier
Fully managed hosting and SuiteASSURED support packages are available for teams without in-house IT
cons: Self-hosting requires technical resources to install, maintain and secure
Professional support, implementation (Quick Start) and SuiteASSURED plans cost extra on top of the free software
Community support tier is limited to forums and documentation only

faqs.question: Is SuiteCRM free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core SuiteCRM software is free and open source. You pay only if you choose managed hosting, implementation services or a support package.

faqs.question: Can SuiteCRM be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, SuiteCRM can be downloaded and self-hosted on your own infrastructure at no software cost.

faqs.question: Does SuiteCRM offer hosted plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, SuiteCRM Hosted provides fully managed cloud hosting starting from £50 per month, including a free trial option.

faqs.question: What is SuiteASSURED?
faqs.answer: SuiteASSURED is a paid support package, from £3,350 per year, that includes warranty, legal protections, priority support and care hours.

use_cases.title: Salesforce alternative for cost-conscious teams
use_cases.description: Organizations seeking CRM functionality similar to commercial platforms without per-user licensing fees.
use_cases.audience: SMBs and enterprises with budget constraints

use_cases.title: Self-hosted CRM with custom integrations
use_cases.description: Technical teams that want full control of their CRM data and infrastructure, using the open REST API to connect other business systems.

use_cases.title: Managed CRM for teams without IT staff
use_cases.description: Businesses that want SuiteCRM's functionality without managing servers, using the fully managed hosting option.
use_cases.audience: Small businesses without dedicated IT resources

features: You provide and manage your own infrastructure
Best for technical teams with in-house IT
Hosting, security, updates and backups included
Best for teams without in-house IT resources
Configuration and one day of training
Best for most teams getting started
20% discount when bundled with managed hosting

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SuiteCRM?
paragraphs: SuiteCRM is an open-source customer relationship management platform built to give organizations a 360-degree view of customers and business operations. It covers sales, marketing and customer service through a flexible data model, reporting dashboards, lead and opportunity management, and automated segmentation.

The software itself is free to download and self-host, with no per-user licensing fees. SuiteCRM Ltd, which develops the platform, also sells optional managed hosting, implementation and support packages, including a warranty-backed SuiteASSURED plan, for teams that want additional assurance or don't have in-house IT.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SuiteCRM is for
paragraphs: SuiteCRM suits organizations looking for a customizable, lower-cost alternative to proprietary CRM platforms, from technical teams that want to self-host and extend the open REST API, to businesses that prefer fully managed hosting and professional support instead of running their own servers.

enrichment.short_description: EspoCRM is an open-source CRM for managing contacts, companies and opportunities, available self-hosted or as a paid cloud service.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: EspoCRM: open-source CRM software, self-hosted for free or on Basic, Enterprise and Ultimate cloud plans from €12.90/user/month.

features.title: Contact and company management
features.description: Track contacts, companies, projects and opportunities in one place.

features.title: Self-hosted or cloud deployment
features.description: Install on Windows, macOS or Linux via Docker, or run on EspoCRM's cloud hosted on Google Cloud, AWS or Azure.

features.title: API access
features.description: Connect EspoCRM to other systems through its API.

features.title: 2,000+ integrations
features.description: Cloud plans include access to over 2,000 third-party integrations.

features.title: Advanced Pack extensions
features.description: All cloud plans include nine official extensions such as Sales Pack, Project Management, Google Integration and Outlook Integration at no extra cost.

features.title: 12x5 support
features.description: Cloud plans include support during business hours across the week.

pros: Free, self-hosted open-source edition with no license cost
Used by over 50,000 companies across 163 countries
Cloud plans include 2,000+ integrations and the Advanced Pack extensions at no extra charge
30-day free trial available on cloud plans
Runs on Windows, macOS and Linux via Docker for self-hosting
cons: Cloud plans require a minimum number of users (3 for Basic, 5 for Enterprise, 10 for Ultimate)
Cloud pricing is billed for 6 months or a year, not month-to-month
Record limits on the Basic and Enterprise cloud plans require upgrading or paying extra to extend
Self-hosting requires managing your own server infrastructure

faqs.question: Is EspoCRM free?
faqs.answer: Yes, EspoCRM can be self-hosted for free as open-source software. EspoCRM also sells paid cloud hosting plans.

faqs.question: What does EspoCRM Cloud cost?
faqs.answer: Cloud plans start at €12.90 per user per month (Basic, minimum 3 users), up to €59 per user per month for the Ultimate plan.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, EspoCRM cloud plans include a 30-day free trial.

faqs.question: Can EspoCRM be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, EspoCRM can be installed on your own Windows, macOS or Linux server, including via Docker.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted CRM for technical teams
use_cases.description: Organizations that want to run EspoCRM on their own infrastructure at no license cost.

use_cases.title: Managed cloud CRM for growing teams
use_cases.description: Businesses that prefer EspoCRM's cloud hosting on Google Cloud, AWS or Azure over self-managing servers.
use_cases.audience: Sales, retail, real estate, education and healthcare teams

use_cases.title: Multi-department customer management
use_cases.description: Companies in wholesale, e-commerce, banking, call centers and manufacturing tracking customer relationships across departments.

features: Dedicated IP, private server and custom domain

long_form_blocks.heading: What is EspoCRM?
paragraphs: EspoCRM is a web-based CRM application for tracking contacts, companies, projects and opportunities. It is open source and can be self-hosted for free on Windows, macOS or Linux, including via Docker.

EspoCRM also offers cloud-hosted plans on Google Cloud, AWS or Azure, priced per user per month across Basic, Enterprise and Ultimate tiers. All cloud plans include API access, over 2,000 integrations and a set of official extensions at no extra cost.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who EspoCRM is for
paragraphs: EspoCRM is used across industries including wholesale, e-commerce, banking, call centers, manufacturing, education, travel, real estate, healthcare and insurance, by teams that want either a free self-hosted CRM or a managed cloud alternative.

enrichment.short_description: YetiForce is a free, open-source CRM/ERP system with 80+ modules for sales, marketing, support, finance and HR.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: YetiForce: free open-source CRM/ERP with 80+ modules for sales, marketing, support, finance, logistics and HR, used by 23,000+ firms.

features.title: 80+ ready-made modules
features.description: Modules cover marketing, sales, project management, customer support, finance, logistics, HR and office administration.

features.title: Dynamic report builder
features.description: Build custom reports and analytics without external tools.

features.title: Invoicing
features.description: Generate invoices directly within the system.

features.title: CRM and ERP in one system
features.description: Combines customer relationship management with enterprise resource planning capabilities.

features.title: Self-hosted, open-source deployment
features.description: Install and customize YetiForce on your own infrastructure at no software cost.

pros: Completely free and open-source with no license fees
Broad functionality with 80+ modules spanning CRM and ERP
Used by roughly 23,000 firms across 100 countries
Proven at scale, with a largest deployment of 12,500 users
Recognized on review platforms including Capterra, G2 and SourceForge
cons: Optional paid support and subscription services are needed for enterprise-level assistance
Self-hosted deployment requires technical setup and maintenance
Official site discloses limited detail on specific third-party integrations

faqs.question: Is YetiForce free?
faqs.answer: Yes, YetiForce is free, open-source software. Paid support and subscription services are available separately through its marketplace.

faqs.question: Is YetiForce a CRM or an ERP?
faqs.answer: YetiForce combines both, with 80+ modules covering CRM functions like sales and marketing alongside ERP functions like finance and HR.

faqs.question: Who develops YetiForce?
faqs.answer: YetiForce is developed and maintained by Stohid Technology S.A., based in Warsaw, Poland.

faqs.question: How many organizations use YetiForce?
faqs.answer: YetiForce reports around 23,000 firms using the platform across 100 countries, with a largest single deployment of 12,500 users.

use_cases.title: Combined CRM/ERP for growing businesses
use_cases.description: Organizations that want sales, marketing, finance and HR modules in one open-source system.

use_cases.title: Large-scale self-hosted deployments
use_cases.description: Companies needing a system proven to scale to thousands of users, as demonstrated by YetiForce's largest deployment.

use_cases.title: Cost-sensitive organizations
use_cases.description: Businesses that want extensive CRM/ERP functionality without ongoing license fees.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is YetiForce?
paragraphs: YetiForce is a free, open-source CRM/ERP system built by Stohid Technology S.A. in Warsaw, Poland. It ships with 80+ ready-made modules covering marketing, sales, project management, customer support, finance, logistics, HR and office administration, plus a dynamic report builder and built-in invoicing.

The core software is open source and self-hosted at no license cost. Stohid Technology also offers optional paid support and subscription services through a marketplace for organizations that want additional assistance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who YetiForce is for
paragraphs: YetiForce fits businesses of any size looking for a single free system that covers both CRM and ERP needs, from small teams to large organizations, with reported deployments as large as 12,500 users at a single firm.

enrichment.short_description: OroCRM is an open-source, Symfony-based CRM with a free Community Edition and a paid Enterprise edition.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OroCRM: open-source Symfony-based CRM with a free Community Edition and a paid Enterprise edition for larger organizations.

features.title: 360-degree customer view
features.description: Track customer interactions and sales performance across the business.

features.title: Contact, lead and opportunity management
features.description: Manage the sales pipeline from first contact through to closed opportunity.

features.title: Workflow automation
features.description: Automate business processes and task management within the CRM.

features.title: Customizable reporting and dashboards
features.description: Build reports and dashboards tailored to specific business metrics.

features.title: Built on Symfony
features.description: OroCRM is built on the Symfony PHP framework, allowing deep customization and extension.

features.title: Open-source Community Edition
features.description: The Community Edition is fully functional and licensed under OSL-3.0, distributed as source code on GitHub.

pros: Free, open-source Community Edition with source code publicly available on GitHub
Built on the established Symfony PHP framework, enabling deep customization
Designed with flexibility in mind for businesses with specific CRM requirements
An Enterprise edition is available for organizations that need additional capabilities
cons: Enterprise edition pricing is not publicly disclosed on Oro's official channels
Requires PHP/Symfony technical expertise to deploy, customize and maintain
Public documentation on feature differences between Community and Enterprise editions is limited

faqs.question: Is OroCRM free?
faqs.answer: The OroCRM Community Edition is free and open source, licensed under OSL-3.0. Oro also offers a paid Enterprise edition.

faqs.question: What is OroCRM built on?
faqs.answer: OroCRM is built on the Symfony PHP framework.

faqs.question: Is OroCRM's source code available?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Edition source code is published on GitHub under oroinc/crm-application.

faqs.question: Is there an Enterprise version of OroCRM?
faqs.answer: Yes, Oro offers an Enterprise edition alongside the free Community Edition, though enterprise pricing is not publicly listed.

use_cases.title: Developer-customized CRM
use_cases.description: Development teams that want to build on a Symfony-based open-source CRM foundation and extend it to fit specific workflows.

use_cases.title: B2B-focused customer management
use_cases.description: Businesses wanting an extensible CRM for tracking contacts, leads and opportunities.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OroCRM?
paragraphs: OroCRM is an open-source customer relationship management application built on the Symfony PHP framework. Its Community Edition is free, fully functional, and distributed as source code on GitHub under the OSL-3.0 license, giving businesses the ability to customize and extend the CRM to fit specific needs.

Alongside the free Community Edition, Oro also offers a paid Enterprise edition for organizations that need additional capabilities, though detailed public pricing for the Enterprise tier is not published on Oro's official channels.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OroCRM is for
paragraphs: OroCRM suits development teams comfortable working with PHP and Symfony who want an open-source CRM they can deeply customize, as well as businesses considering the paid Enterprise edition for additional support.

enrichment.short_description: Monica is an open-source personal CRM for tracking relationships, self-hosted for free or hosted for $9/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Monica: open-source personal CRM to track relationships, contacts and reminders. Free plan, $9/month unlimited plan, or self-host for free.

features.title: Contact and relationship management
features.description: Organize information about family, friends and other personal connections.

features.title: Activity logging and reminders
features.description: Log activities with contacts and set reminders for important dates.

features.title: Journal
features.description: Keep a personal journal alongside contact records.

features.title: Personal details tracking
features.description: Store details like gift ideas, tasks, debts, pets and work information for each contact.

features.title: Self-hosting option
features.description: Install Monica's open-source code on your own server at no cost.

pros: Open-source code is publicly available and can be self-hosted for free
Paid plan uses flat pricing with unlimited contacts and no per-contact fees
No advertising, and the company states it does not exploit user data
Cancel anytime with no cancellation fees
Free hosted plan requires no credit card to sign up
cons: Free hosted plan is limited to 10 contacts
Some features are restricted on the free tier
Users wanting full privacy without a subscription must self-host, which requires technical setup

faqs.question: Is Monica free?
faqs.answer: Monica has a free hosted plan limited to 10 contacts, and the open-source code can be self-hosted for free without limits. A paid hosted plan removes the contact limit.

faqs.question: How much does Monica cost?
faqs.answer: The paid hosted plan is $9/month or $90/year, with unlimited contacts and reminders at a flat rate.

faqs.question: Can I cancel anytime?
faqs.answer: Yes, Monica states there are no commitments and you can cancel anytime with no cancellation fees.

faqs.question: Can Monica be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, Monica's source code is open source and available on GitHub for self-hosting.

use_cases.title: Tracking personal relationships
use_cases.description: Individuals who want to remember important details and milestones about family and friends.

use_cases.title: Privacy-focused personal CRM
use_cases.description: Users who prefer to self-host their personal relationship data rather than use a hosted service.

use_cases.title: Managing a large personal network
use_cases.description: People with many contacts who want unlimited contacts and reminders at a flat monthly rate.
use_cases.audience: Networkers and people with large personal circles

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Monica?
paragraphs: Monica is an open-source personal CRM for organizing information about the people in your life, from family and friends to broader personal connections. It supports contact management, activity logging, reminders, and a personal journal.

Monica offers a free hosted plan limited to 10 contacts, a paid hosted plan at $9/month (or $90/year) with unlimited contacts and no per-contact fees, and a self-hosted option using the open-source code, which is free to run on your own server.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Monica is for
paragraphs: Monica is built for individuals who want to keep track of relationships and personal details without advertising or data monetization, whether they choose the affordable hosted plan or self-host for full control.

enrichment.short_description: Dolibarr is a free, open-source ERP and CRM suite for managing sales, HR, finance, inventory and projects.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Dolibarr: free open-source ERP/CRM suite covering sales, HR, finance, inventory and projects for SMEs, freelancers and associations.

features.title: CRM and sales
features.description: Manage customers, proposals, orders, contracts and a help desk.

features.title: HR management
features.description: Handle employee records, expense reports, leave requests and timesheets.

features.title: Finance
features.description: Manage billing, accounting and bank reconciliation.

features.title: Inventory
features.description: Track products, stock, purchasing and manufacturing.

features.title: Productivity tools
features.description: Manage projects, tasks, interventions and an agenda.

features.title: APIs and import/export
features.description: Integrate with other systems and move data in and out of Dolibarr.

pros: Free, open-source ERP and CRM suite with no license fees
Modular design lets organizations enable only the features they need
Runs on Windows, Mac, Linux, Docker and cloud deployments
Backed by a large international community of developers, translators and testers
Dolibarr Association, founded in 2009, oversees the project
cons: Paid hosting and support are only available through third-party partners, not Dolibarr directly
Self-hosted deployment requires technical setup and maintenance
No pricing details for partner-provided hosting or support are published on the official site

faqs.question: Is Dolibarr free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Dolibarr is free open-source software. Optional paid hosting and support are available through partner companies.

faqs.question: What does Dolibarr cover?
faqs.answer: Dolibarr covers CRM and sales, HR, finance, inventory and productivity in one integrated suite.

faqs.question: What platforms does Dolibarr support?
faqs.answer: Dolibarr supports Windows, Mac and Linux, as well as Docker and cloud deployments.

faqs.question: Who maintains Dolibarr?
faqs.answer: The Dolibarr Association, founded in 2009, promotes and oversees the open-source project.

use_cases.title: All-in-one ERP/CRM for SMEs
use_cases.description: Small and medium-sized businesses wanting a free, integrated system covering sales, finance and inventory.

use_cases.title: Free invoicing and CRM for freelancers
use_cases.description: Independent professionals needing billing, proposals and customer tracking without licensing costs.

use_cases.title: Low-cost management for associations
use_cases.description: Nonprofits and associations needing an integrated management tool on a limited budget.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Dolibarr?
paragraphs: Dolibarr is a free, open-source ERP and CRM suite that brings together customer management, HR, finance, inventory and project tools in one web application. It is overseen by the Dolibarr Association, founded in 2009.

The software is free to download and self-host on Windows, Mac, Linux, Docker or the cloud. Paid hosting and support are available, but only through third-party partner companies rather than Dolibarr itself.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Dolibarr is for
paragraphs: Dolibarr is designed for SMEs, large companies, freelancers and associations that want an integrated, free ERP and CRM system they can run themselves or deploy with a hosting partner.

enrichment.short_description: ERPNext is a free, open-source ERP covering accounting, sales, inventory, manufacturing and HR, with paid Frappe Cloud hosting.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ERPNext: free open-source ERP for accounting, sales, inventory, manufacturing and HR, or hosted on Frappe Cloud from $14/month.

features.title: Accounting
features.description: General ledger, multi-currency support, financial statements, fixed assets and tax compliance.

features.title: Procurement
features.description: Purchase orders, supplier management and multi-level approvals.

features.title: Sales and invoicing
features.description: Order-to-cash workflows, pricing rules and payment processing.

features.title: CRM
features.description: Lead and opportunity management, quotations and SLA management.

features.title: Inventory
features.description: Item tracking, warehouses, and serial/batch management.

features.title: Manufacturing
features.description: Bill of materials, production planning, quality checks and subcontracting.

features.title: HR and payroll
features.description: People operations management alongside other ERP modules.

features.title: No-code/low-code customization
features.description: Customize the system without extensive coding, with a mobile-friendly interface.

pros: Self-hosted edition is free and open source with no per-user licensing
Frappe Cloud managed hosting is available starting at $14/month
Broad module coverage from accounting to manufacturing to HR in a single platform
200+ certified implementation partners globally
Adopted by 30,000+ companies worldwide
Described as 100% mobile-friendly across modules
cons: Self-hosting requires technical setup and customization knowledge
Frappe Cloud pricing is compute-based rather than a flat per-user fee, which can make costs less predictable
Enterprise-level support and some integrations may require engaging a certified partner

faqs.question: Is ERPNext free?
faqs.answer: Yes, ERPNext can be self-hosted for free as open-source software. Frappe Cloud offers paid managed hosting starting at $14/month.

faqs.question: Who develops ERPNext?
faqs.answer: ERPNext is developed and maintained by Frappe Technologies.

faqs.question: Does ERPNext charge per user?
faqs.answer: No, ERPNext does not use per-user pricing; Frappe Cloud pricing is based on compute resources instead.

faqs.question: How many companies use ERPNext?
faqs.answer: ERPNext reports more than 30,000 companies using the platform globally.

use_cases.title: Free all-in-one ERP for SMBs
use_cases.description: Small and medium businesses wanting accounting, sales, inventory and HR in one open-source system.

use_cases.title: Manufacturing planning and quality control
use_cases.description: Manufacturers needing bill of materials, production planning and quality checks.

use_cases.title: Cost-effective ERP for nonprofits and education
use_cases.description: Organizations wanting vendor independence and lower ERP costs compared to proprietary systems.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ERPNext?
paragraphs: ERPNext is a free and open-source ERP developed by Frappe Technologies, covering accounting, procurement, sales, CRM, inventory, manufacturing, projects, point of sale, HR/payroll, quality management and helpdesk functions in a single platform.

ERPNext can be self-hosted at no cost, or run on Frappe Cloud, the company's managed hosting service, with compute-based pricing starting at $14/month and scaling to $108/month depending on usage. Frappe Technologies also supports a network of 200+ certified implementation partners.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ERPNext is for
paragraphs: ERPNext serves SMBs and enterprises across manufacturing, trading, retail, healthcare, education, professional services and nonprofit sectors that want a cost-effective, customizable ERP without per-user SaaS pricing.

enrichment.short_description: Tryton is a 100% free and open-source, modular ERP covering accounting, sales, inventory, CRM and manufacturing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Tryton: 100% free, open-source modular ERP with accounting, sales, inventory, CRM and manufacturing, governed by a Foundation.

features.title: Financial and analytic accounting
features.description: Manage general and analytic accounting within the platform.

features.title: Sales order management
features.description: Handle sales orders as part of an integrated workflow.

features.title: Inventory and stock tracking
features.description: Track stock levels and inventory movements.

features.title: CRM
features.description: Manage customer relationships alongside other business operations.

features.title: Purchasing and supply chain
features.description: Manage purchasing and supply chain operations.

features.title: Manufacturing with bill of materials
features.description: Support manufacturing processes using bills of materials.

features.title: Project management
features.description: Manage projects within the same integrated system.

features.title: Third-party integrations
features.description: Automate communication with banks, carriers and websites.

pros: 100% free and open source with no vendor lock-in
Fully modular, so businesses can use only the components they need
Governed by an independent Foundation rather than a single vendor
Network of independent service providers available for support and implementation
Distributed under CC BY-SA 3.0 with a protected trademark
cons: No official hosted/cloud offering is advertised, so deployment is self-managed
Support comes only through third-party service providers rather than a central vendor
The official site provides no user or customer statistics to gauge adoption scale

faqs.question: Is Tryton free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Tryton is described as 100% open source and free to install and deploy.

faqs.question: Who governs Tryton?
faqs.answer: Tryton is governed by a Foundation with a board, supporters and a network of service providers.

faqs.question: What license does Tryton use?
faqs.answer: Tryton is distributed under the CC BY-SA 3.0 license, with a protected trademark.

faqs.question: Does Tryton include CRM?
faqs.answer: Yes, Tryton includes a CRM module alongside accounting, sales, inventory and manufacturing modules.

use_cases.title: Modular open-source ERP for any size business
use_cases.description: Companies wanting to enable only the accounting, sales, inventory or manufacturing modules they need.

use_cases.title: Avoiding vendor lock-in
use_cases.description: Organizations that want an ERP governed by a Foundation rather than a single commercial vendor.

use_cases.title: Integrated accounting, CRM and manufacturing
use_cases.description: Companies needing accounting, customer management and manufacturing in one connected system.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Tryton?
paragraphs: Tryton is a 100% open-source, modular business application suite covering financial and analytic accounting, sales order management, inventory and stock tracking, CRM, purchasing, manufacturing with bill of materials, project management and third-party integrations with banks and carriers.

The project is governed by an independent Foundation, distributed under the CC BY-SA 3.0 license with a protected trademark, and supported by a network of independent service providers rather than a single vendor.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Tryton is for
paragraphs: Tryton fits companies of any size that want comprehensive, integrated business management software without proprietary licensing costs or vendor lock-in.

enrichment.short_description: metasfresh is a Fraunhofer-validated cloud ERP for the DACH region, covering supply chain, production and billing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: metasfresh: Fraunhofer-validated cloud ERP for DACH-region SMEs, covering supply chain, production and billing, from €79/user/month.

features.title: Planning and demand forecasting
features.description: Plan operations and forecast demand across the supply chain.

features.title: Procurement and goods receipt
features.description: Manage purchasing and incoming goods.

features.title: Production and warehouse logistics
features.description: Coordinate production runs alongside warehouse logistics.

features.title: Shipping and distribution
features.description: Manage outbound shipping and distribution processes.

features.title: Billing and traceability
features.description: Handle invoicing with traceability across the supply chain.

features.title: German-hosted cloud infrastructure
features.description: Runs as cloud-based SaaS hosted in German data centers.

pros: Fraunhofer IML-validated since March 2022, an independent research institute endorsement
Built on an open-source foundation with proprietary support and hosting services
Strong DACH-region focus with German data center hosting
Integrates with DATEV accounting and established EDI/B2B and GS1 partners
States sustainability commitments including renewable energy and carbon offsetting
cons: Pricing is relatively high for smaller businesses, at €79-99/user/month plus €249-299/month hosting (excl. VAT)
Primarily focused on Germany, Austria and Switzerland, which may limit fit elsewhere
Starter package requires a custom implementation quote rather than transparent self-serve pricing

faqs.question: Is metasfresh open source?
faqs.answer: metasfresh is built on an open-source foundation, but the hosted product is sold as a paid cloud ERP with user and hosting fees.

faqs.question: How much does metasfresh cost?
faqs.answer: User fees run €79-99/month per user, plus cloud hosting from €249-299/month, all excluding VAT, alongside a custom-quoted Starter implementation package.

faqs.question: Who is metasfresh for?
faqs.answer: metasfresh targets small-to-medium enterprises in the DACH region, including food industry, wholesale and green industry (agriculture/horticulture) companies.

faqs.question: Has metasfresh been independently validated?
faqs.answer: Yes, metasfresh has been Fraunhofer IML-validated since March 2022.

use_cases.title: Supply chain traceability for food and wholesale
use_cases.description: Companies in the food industry and wholesale needing traceability from procurement through billing.

use_cases.title: Agriculture and horticulture ERP
use_cases.description: Green industry businesses needing supply chain and production management.

use_cases.title: German-compliant hosted ERP
use_cases.description: DACH-region SMEs wanting Fraunhofer-validated ERP with local data hosting and DATEV integration.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is metasfresh?
paragraphs: metasfresh is a cloud-based ERP built on open-source technology and operated by metas GmbH, focused on mid-sized companies in the DACH region (Germany, Austria, Switzerland). It covers the full supply chain: planning and demand forecasting, procurement and goods receipt, production and warehouse logistics, shipping and distribution, and billing with traceability.

The system has been Fraunhofer IML-validated since March 2022. Pricing is a hybrid model: a custom-quoted Starter implementation package, plus ongoing user fees of €79-99/month and cloud hosting from €249-299/month, hosted in German data centers, all excluding VAT.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who metasfresh is for
paragraphs: metasfresh is aimed at small-to-medium enterprises in the DACH region, particularly in the food industry, wholesale and green industry (agriculture/horticulture) sectors that need supply chain automation with German-hosted infrastructure.

enrichment.short_description: Axelor is a low-code/no-code open-source business suite combining ERP, CRM, BPM and BI, with an AI module supporting multiple providers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Axelor: open-source low-code ERP/CRM/BPM suite with a free Community edition and paid Pro/Enterprise tiers with AI and advanced BI.

features.title: Studio
features.description: Drag-and-drop customization of the application without coding.

features.title: BPM (Business Process Management)
features.description: No-code workflow automation for business processes.

features.title: Business Intelligence
features.description: Interactive reports and dashboards built into the platform.

features.title: Axelor Connect
features.description: Integrate with 2,000+ third-party applications.

features.title: Axelor AI
features.description: AI integration across processes, with support for OpenAI, Mistral, Anthropic, Google and other model providers (Enterprise tier).

features.title: Portal
features.description: Customer collaboration and communication tools.

features.title: 50+ business applications
features.description: Covers accounting, HR, sales, procurement, production and more in one suite.

pros: Free, open-source Community edition available for download
No-code Studio and BPM tools reduce the need for custom development
AI module supports multiple LLM providers including OpenAI, Mistral, Anthropic and Google
Axelor Connect offers integration with 2,000+ applications
ISO 27001 certified with a global presence across 7 agencies on 4 continents
Reports over 1 million users, from SMBs to large enterprises such as Trenitalia, Stellantis and ArcelorMittal
cons: Pro and Enterprise pricing is not public and requires contacting sales for a quote
Pro plans require a minimum of 10 users and Enterprise plans a minimum of 20 users
Advanced features such as SSO, advanced BI and the integrated AI module are reserved for the Enterprise tier

faqs.question: Is Axelor free?
faqs.answer: Axelor offers a free, open-source Community edition. Pro and Enterprise editions are paid and require a custom quote.

faqs.question: What's the difference between Axelor's plans?
faqs.answer: Community is free and open source; Pro (minimum 10 users) adds all business modules and standard support; Enterprise (minimum 20 users) adds SSO, advanced BI, an integrated AI module and priority support.

faqs.question: Does Axelor support AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Axelor AI module integrates with providers including OpenAI, Mistral, Anthropic and Google, and is included in the Enterprise tier.

faqs.question: How many people use Axelor?
faqs.answer: Axelor reports over 1 million users ranging from small and medium businesses to large enterprises.

use_cases.title: Free open-source ERP/CRM/BPM
use_cases.description: SMEs wanting a no-cost, unified ERP, CRM and business process management platform.

use_cases.title: Vertical solutions for regulated industries
use_cases.description: Manufacturing, construction and public sector organizations needing tailored business applications.
use_cases.audience: Manufacturing, construction and public sector organizations

use_cases.title: No-code process automation with AI
use_cases.description: Enterprises wanting to automate workflows using Studio and BPM, enhanced with the AI module.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Axelor?
paragraphs: Axelor is an open-source business application suite that combines ERP, CRM, BPM, business intelligence and web portals in one platform. It includes Studio for drag-and-drop customization, no-code BPM workflow automation, and Axelor Connect for integrating with 2,000+ applications.

Axelor is offered in three tiers: a free open-source Community edition, a Pro edition for a minimum of 10 users, and an Enterprise edition for a minimum of 20 users that adds SSO, advanced business intelligence and the Axelor AI module, which supports providers including OpenAI, Mistral, Anthropic and Google.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Axelor is for
paragraphs: Axelor serves organizations across manufacturing, professional services, construction, public sector and distribution, from SMEs using the free Community edition to large enterprises such as Trenitalia, Stellantis and ArcelorMittal on paid Pro or Enterprise plans.
